name: Stuffed Chicken Breasts
recipeIngredient: 20 ounces Frozen spinach, thawed and drained
2 tablespoons Fresh herbs (Oregano, summer savory, Chervil, etc.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onions and sauté until soft.
2. In a separate bowl, combine the thawed spinach, ricotta cheese, beaten eggs, parsley, fresh herbs, salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
3. Stir in the cooked onions to mix well with all ingredients.
4. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
5. Place each chicken breast on a cutting board, skin side up. Trim excess fat if necessary.
6. Loosen the skin from one side of the breast and stuff about 1/3 cup of the filling under the skin.
7. Tuck the skin and meat underneath to form an even, round dome shape.
8. Place the stuffed breasts in a buttered baking dish.
9. Bake for 15 minutes before placing in the oven at 350°F (175°C).
10. Cook until golden brown, about 30-35 minutes. Do not overcook; chicken will be dry if cooked too long.
11. Allow to cool slightly before serving. If desired, let cool to room temperature before slicing into smaller pieces.
12. Arrange on platters and garnish with fresh herbs.
